title: Dysoxylum pilosum
intro: Dysoxylum pilosum, also known as the Philippine Dysoxylum, is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, which can aid in treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Dysoxylum pilosum include flavonoids, tannins, and alkaloids, particularly the quassinoid compounds that exhibit significant pharmacological activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in folk medicine to treat fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Dysoxylum pilosum belongs to the family Meliaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the mahogany family. This family includes over 300 species of trees and shrubs, many of which are native to tropical regions. Dysoxylum pilosum, specifically, is a species of tree that is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The tree is classified as a member of the genus Dysoxylum, which includes around 60 species of trees and shrubs. The classification of Dysoxylum pilosum reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Meliaceae family.
uses: Dysoxylum pilosum is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat fever and other illnesses. The bark and leaves of Dysoxylum pilosum are used to make medicinal concoctions. Additionally, the plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's full potential in treating various health issues.
